questions.question: Describe the main difference between a block code and a cyclic code in the context of data link layer error detection and correction. (5 marks)
questions.hint: Block codes divide data into fixed length blocks, whereas cyclic codes divide data into cyclic blocks.

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of using cyclic codes over block codes for data transmission? (5 marks)
questions.hint: Cyclic codes use fewer bits to encode data compared to block codes.

questions.question: Explain the process of parity-checking in cyclic codes. How is it used to detect errors in received data? (10 marks)
questions.hint: Parity-checking involves dividing the received data into cyclic blocks and checking the parity bits. If the parity bits do not match the calculated parity bits, an error is detected.
